what-is-fov.question: What is field of view in photography?
what-is-fov.answer: Field of view (FOV) is the angular extent of the scene visible through your lens and camera sensor. It determines how much of a scene is captured in a photo and is affected by focal length and sensor size.

how-crop-factor-affects-fov.question: How does crop factor affect field of view?
how-crop-factor-affects-fov.answer: A smaller sensor (higher crop factor) captures a narrower field of view than a full-frame sensor at the same focal length. For example, APS-C sensors with a 1.5x crop factor make a 50mm lens behave like a 75mm lens in terms of field of view.

fov-vs-focal-length.question: What is the relationship between FOV and focal length?
fov-vs-focal-length.answer: Field of view and focal length are inversely related. Shorter focal lengths (wide-angle) give a wider field of view, while longer focal lengths (telephoto) give a narrower field of view. The exact FOV also depends on the sensor size.
